===============================================================================

**Job Description**
-------------------

  

  

**About the Role:**
===================

12
The DevSecOps Engineer – Artifact Management \& Software Supply Chain Security focuses on securing and governing enterprise artifact and dependency management platforms. This role combines DevSecOps, application security, and cloud security to ensure that build artifacts and dependencies are trusted, curated, and consumed securely across CI/CD pipelines and cloud environments.


**Key Responsibilities**


* Design, deploy, and operate enterprise artifact repository platforms supporting cloud and hybrid environments.
* Define and enforce package curation, promotion, and trust models aligned with application security and compliance requirements.
* Implement and govern waiver and approval workflows for dependency and artifact usage, ensuring risk\-based decision‑making.
* Partner with AppSec, platform, and engineering teams to standardize secure dependency and artifact consumption patterns.
* Define and maintain repository architectures supporting multiple environments, teams, and trust boundaries.
* Enforce policies ensuring artifact immutability, provenance, versioning, and trusted sourcing.
* Integrate artifact repositories into CI/CD pipelines built on GitHub, Jenkins, and Azure DevOps.
* Embed security controls for AI/ML and GenAI workloads within CI/CD pipelines and developer workflows.
* Define and enforce secure usage patterns for LLMs and AI services, including prompt handling, data protection, and model access controls.
* Implement safeguards against AI\-specific threats, including prompt injection, model poisoning, data leakage, and insecure model outputs.
* Integrate AI security scanning and validation into build pipelines, ensuring safe model usage and dependency integrity.
* Collaborate with engineering teams to establish secure\-by\-design AI application architectures.
* Ensure compliance with enterprise Responsible AI policies (data privacy, bias management, model governance).
* Secure AI\-related secrets, tokens, and API access used in pipelines and applications.
* Monitor and respond to security risks introduced by AI/ML components, including third\-party models and APIs.
* Contribute to AI risk governance, auditability, and traceability across the SDLC.
* Stay current on emerging AI security threats, vulnerabilities, and regulatory expectations.
* Align artifact and dependency controls with cloud security best practices for deployed applications.
* Monitor usage, risk posture, and effectiveness of artifact controls and drive continuous improvement.
* Develop automation and policy‑as‑code for artifact lifecycle management, approvals, and governance.
* Support security incident investigations related to software supply chain integrity or dependency risk.
* Create documentation, standards, and enablement materials for secure developer adoption.

**Required Qualifications**


* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Cybersecurity, or equivalent experience.
* 3–6 years of experience in DevSecOps, platform security, or software supply chain security.
* Strong hands\-on experience with JFrog Artifactory, including deployment and enterprise architecture.
* Experience designing package curation and promotion models.
* Foundational understanding of AI/ML and Generative AI concepts, including LLMs and model lifecycle.
* Knowledge of AI/ML security risks such as prompt injection, data poisoning, model evasion, and data leakage.
* Experience integrating AI or ML components into applications or pipelines (preferred hands\-on exposure).
* Familiarity with Responsible AI principles and AI governance framewor

jobs.title: Full Stack Power BI Report Server & Lead Implementation Engineer
jobs.description:
**\#\#\# \*\*Job Description\*\***

We are seeking a sharp, highly execution\-focused Full Stack Power BI Report Server \& Lead Implementation Engineer to join our tight\-knit, 7\-person team. This role is uniquely designed for an engineer who thrives on optimizing architecture, writing clean production code, and implementing advanced BI systems without the friction of traditional corporate translation layers.

\#\#\# **\*\*Our Workflow: AI\-Accelerated Prototyping to Production Execution\*\***

In our company, the business owner acts as the Principal Product Architect—defining business rules directly and utilizing AI tools to generate highly functional prototypes, logic models, and proof\-of\-concept tools. This eliminates the traditional requirements translation phase.

**Your mission is two\-fold:**

1\. \***\*The Partnership:\***\* You will collaborate directly with the owner to help refine the underlying technical frameworks, defining exactly \*how\* the AI should be prompted, structured, and specified to cleanly match our architecture and data models.

2\. **\*\*The Execution:\*\*** You will take these AI\-generated prototypes and transform them into bulletproof, production\-grade reality. You will refactor code for strict type\-safety, optimize database performance, wrap everything in robust HIPAA security, and deploy it to our live ecosystem.

\#\#\# **\*\*Core Responsibilities\*\***

\* **\*\*AI Specification Partnership:\*\*** Collaborate with the Product Architect to optimize the AI prototyping workflow. Help define schemas, input constraints, and technical models so that the AI\-generated logic drops seamlessly into our tech stack.

\* **\*\*Reporting Overhaul \& Architecture:\*\*** Take absolute ownership of our internal reporting ecosystem. Completely overhaul and modernize our reporting layer by migrating legacy Excel, XML, and SSRS data streams into high\-performance dashboards strictly within Power BI Report Server (PBIRS).

\* **\*\*Production Implementation:\*\*** Refactor, secure, and integrate AI\-generated code snippets and layout models into our active full\-stack application (.NET Core / C\# backend and a TypeScript, JavaScript, jQuery, and Bootstrap frontend).

\* **\*\*SQL Server 2025 Migration:\*\*** Lead our database upgrade to SQL Server 2025 Enterprise across our 3\-node, 96\-core failover cluster. **Scale prototypes for new native Vector search and DiskANN indexing into production\-grade features for secure medical records.**

\* **\*\*Cluster Performance Optimization:\*\*** Partner with our DBA to ensure the database engine and the Report Server's background Analysis Services engine (msmdsrv.exe) co\-exist perfectly on our 128 GB RAM nodes by setting strict memory boundaries.

\* **\*\*HIPAA Gatekeeping:\*\*** Ensure all implemented code, database queries, and Active Directory\-linked Row\-Level Security (RLS) configurations strictly safeguard Protected Health Information (PHI).

**\#\#\# \*\*Required Skills \& Qualifications\*\***

\* **\*\*Full\-Stack Competency:\*\*** Strong production experience bridging a .NET Core backend with a frontend utilizing TypeScript, JavaScript, jQuery, and Bootstrap.

\* **\*\*Power BI Report Server:\*\*** Minimum 3 years of hands\-on experience explicitly configuring and deploying PBIRS on\-premises, writing advanced DAX, and modeling data for shared infrastructure.

\* **\*\*Database Mastery:\*\*** Expert\-level T\-SQL, execution plan analysis, and optimization within clustered SQL Server environments.

\* **\*\*Systems Thinking \& Prompt Collaboration:\*\*** Highly comfortable analyzing how AI engines interpret requirements, with the ability to give feedback on how to structure technical specifications for maximum AI output quality.

Pay: $40\.00 \- $50\.00 per hour

* How many years of experience do you have explicitly deploying, configuring, and managing Power BI Report Server on\-premises?

We are looking for a Design Verification Engineer to join our growing team in Hercules, CA United States!

The role primarily focuses on sustaining projects within the clinical diagnostics division, including diabetes instruments. The candidate will be actively involved in both hardware (HW) and software (SW) verification and validation testing.  


**Key Responsibilities:**
* Support test execution, including dry runs and exploratory testing.
* Identify, document, and investigate bugs; create deviation and anomaly reports.
* Develop and maintain test cases and protocols for both HW and SW testing.

* Occasionally handle blood\-based reagents and samples; support for chemistry (blood\-based) tests.

**Preferred Skills:**

**Must Have:**
* Medical Device VnV (manual n automation).

**Nice to have:**

Save your earnings and prepare for your future by enrolling in our 401k plan where Qualitest will match your contributions accelerating your savings plan.

jobs.description:
*This job post is for our NYC location. For our Toronto location job post, go* *here**.*

**Paxos Health is on a mission to help patients access the medical technologies their doctors prescribe.** Too often, even when a physician decides a patient needs a healthcare product (cancer tests, genetic diagnostics, prosthetics, heart valves, neurostimulation devices, home medical equipment, etc.), health insurance companies deny coverage because the evidence, policy, and documentation requirements are complex and inconsistent. Patients don’t get treated, and breakthrough technologies struggle to reach the people they were built to help.


We help healthcare manufacturers fight for coverage patient\-by\-patient. Paxos uses LLMs to turn messy clinical records, payer rules, PDFs, portals, faxes, and reimbursement logic into rigorous prior authorizations and appeals to get patients covered. Before LLMs, this work was too difficult to use AI technology for, and human teams were forced to cut corners to get through all of the patient cases. But now, AI can help manufacturers meet increasingly complex insurer requirements and get more patients access to care.


Our progress so far:

* Five medtech and lab diagnostics companies as customers, with 40\+ companies in pipeline
* $182K in recognized revenue, with line of sight to $1M ARR by late summer
* 9 of our last 10 demo calls came through referrals, word of mouth, or thought leadership
* Raised \>$5M across two VC rounds, including our recently closed Seed
* Won the Stanford Impact Founder and IDIF fellowships. Check out the Stanford article about us and Haley’s LOWkeynotes talk.

**About the role**
------------------


Paxos Health is looking for a hands\-on Founding Applied AI Lead to design and own the AI agents and workflows that make this possible. (Note that the job title itself is flexible based on candidate preferences.)


You’ll sit at the intersection of product, engineering, AI, and customer implementation. You’ll translate messy manual workflows into reliable AI systems that are used in the real world, which includes prompt chains, structured\-output schemas, evals, workflow specs, and implementation logic.


This is a technical workflow\-building role. You should be comfortable with, or able to quickly learn, technical building blocks like JSON, structured outputs, low\-code workflow tools, and lightweight scripting. You won’t need to write production code, but you should be able to prototype, test, and debug AI workflows.


As an early employee, there is a significant opportunity to grow the scope of your role as our company expands.

**Responsibilities**

* **Design and improve Paxos’s AI workflows.** Own the prompt chains, agent patterns, structured\-output schemas, evals, and test cases that power customer implementations.
* **Turn reimbursement complexity into testable workflow logic.** Translate payer rules, document requirements, clinical evidence standards, and operational edge cases into clear AI system behavior.
* **Own AI quality and reliability.** Analyze failures, improve prompts and schemas, and build evals that catch issues before they reach production.
* **Work directly with customers during implementation.** Clarify requirements, understand edge cases, and translate what we learn into workflows and reusable Paxos capabilities.
* **Document how the system works.** Write workflow specs, test plans, prompt/eval documentation, and implementation notes that help the team build and improve faster.


For example, you might turn a payer policy into a structured decision tree, design an eval set for appeal quality, create a reusable prompt pattern for extracting clinical evidence from records, debug why an AI workflow failed on a customer case, and write the implementation spec that engineering and ops use to deploy it.

jobs.title: AI Engineer Intern - GTM / Sales Automation
jobs.description:
**GTM Engineer Intern (AI\-Native)**

**Pronto Networks** · Walnut Creek, CA (On\-site) · Internship · Entry level

**About the role**

GTM Engineering is the fastest\-growing discipline in B2B sales — and almost nobody is teaching it yet. We're hiring a **GTM Engineer Intern** to help build the AI\-native go\-to\-market stack that powers Pronto's sales motion across the US and India.

This isn't a sales internship. You won't be dialing or sending sequences. You'll be **building the systems** that let our BDRs operate 10x more effectively: enrichment workflows, AI agents, automation pipelines, prompt libraries, and data infrastructure. If you're curious about where AI, sales, and software engineering converge, this is the front row.

About Pronto Networks

We design cellular routers, Wi\-Fi access points, LTE/5G gateways, and Wi\-Fi HaLow devices for hospitality, retail, POS, kiosks, healthcare, and smart cities. Our customers include Toast, SpotOn, 365 Retail Markets, Lightspeed Retail, and Elavon. Headquartered in Walnut Creek with a distributed US/India team.

What you'll do

**Build AI workflows** in Claude Cowork, NemoClaw, and n8n — research agents, enrichment pipelines, pre\-call intelligence systems

**Engineer the GTM stack** — connect Apollo, Clay, HubSpot, Instantly, and HeyReach through APIs and automation layers

**Prototype with data** — turn messy Apollo exports and intent signals into tiered, scored, ready\-to\-action account lists

**Develop prompt libraries** — codify what works into reusable, version\-controlled prompts the BDR team can deploy

**Run experiments** — test new AI models, MCP servers, and tools as they ship; document what's worth keeping

**Document and teach** — write internal playbooks so what you build outlives the internship

**What we're looking for**

* Current undergrad or recent grad in CS, engineering, data science, or a quantitative field — or a self\-taught builder with a portfolio that proves it
* Comfortable writing Python or JavaScript; can read API docs and ship a working integration
* Genuine fluency with AI tools — you've built things with Claude, ChatGPT, or Cursor that go beyond chat prompts
* Curious about sales and GTM, even if you've never worked in it — willing to learn the business side
* Strong written communication; you can explain a technical concept to a non\-technical operator
* Self\-directed and able to ship in ambiguity — you don't need a ticket to start work

**Bonus points for:** n8n / Zapier / Make · MCP servers · LangChain or similar agent frameworks · HubSpot or Apollo API experience · prompt engineering portfolio · prior internship at a startup · interest in networking, hardware, or B2B infrastructure

Stack you'll work with

Claude (API \+ Cowork) · NemoClaw · n8n · Apollo · Clay · HubSpot · Instantly · HeyReach · Python · TypeScript · MCP

**What you get**

* Competitive hourly rate
* On\-site at our Walnut Creek HQ (785 Ygnacio Valley Road) — direct collaboration with sales leadership, GTM ops, and engineering
* Real budget for AI tools and APIs — experiment freely
* Ship work that ends up in production, not in a slide deck
* Build a portfolio in the hottest emerging discipline in B2B

**What success looks like**

**First 4 weeks:** Stack onboarded, first automation shipped, prompt library v1 contributed **Mid\-internship:** Owning at least one production workflow used daily by the BDR team **End of internship:** Built systems that measurably improved BDR output; documented well enough that the team keeps using them after you leave

**To apply:** Send a note to careers@prontonetworks.com with (a) something you've built with AI tools and (b) a workflow you'd want to automate at a sales team.

jobs.title: Founding Robotics Engineer
jobs.description:
### **About the Role**


A pre\-seed robotics startup is building a low\-cost, teleoperated humanoid platform designed to bring human intelligence into the physical world — turning physical jobs into remote work. As the **Founding Robotics Engineer**, you will own end\-to\-end design and delivery of the robot, from hardware to software, enabling scalable remote\-work\-powered operations. This is a high\-ownership, high\-impact founding role with meaningful equity and a direct path to shaping manufacturing and deployment at scale.

### **What You'll Do**

* Architect and build a low\-cost teleoperated humanoid robot designed to replace or augment physical on\-site roles.
* Build an initial wheel\-based robot for patient check\-in in urgent care clinics, including self\-charging and safe navigation — all within a Bill of Materials (BOM) under $1,000\.
* Solve the human\-robot interaction challenge: craft an experience that achieves a patient preference win\-rate of \>50% vs. in\-person staff.
* Deploy and maintain a fleet of 50 robots in clinic environments, targeting a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of over 500 hours.
* Ship a contextual AI copilot that provides real\-time guidance to operators, reducing new\-hire training time from weeks to 24 hours — a stepping stone toward a future physical reasoning model.

### **What We're Looking For**

**Required**

* 3\+ years of engineering experience with hands\-on, end\-to\-end ownership of projects.
* **Mechatronics \& Integration:** Proven ability to build robots from scratch, integrating motors, sensors, actuators, and compute into cohesive systems.
* **Embedded Software \& AI:** Reliable firmware development and deployment of ML on edge systems with real\-time, low\-latency constraints.
* **Rapid Prototyping:** Expert in CAD, 3D printing, and quick\-turn fabrication — idea to physical prototype in days.
* **Design for Manufacturing:** Designs with cost, assembly time, tolerances, material selection, and scalable production in mind.
* **Radical Ownership:** You see a problem and start solving it — no permission needed. You own outcomes, not tasks.
* **First\-Principles Thinking:** You question assumptions and invent novel solutions rather than defaulting to existing approaches.
* Strong CS fundamentals supporting both mechatronics and software/hardware integration.

**Nice to Have**

* **Human\-Robot Interaction (HRI) Intuition:** Instinct for what makes robots feel trustworthy vs. unsettling; practical understanding of the uncanny valley.
* **Fleet Management:** Experience deploying and maintaining robot fleets in clinical or field environments with defined uptime targets.
* Exposure to healthcare or clinical environments.
* Early\-stage or founding\-team experience at a startup.

### **Compensation \& Benefits**

* Salary: $120,000 – $140,000 USD annually
* \~1% equity
* Visa sponsorship available
* Approximately 60% hardware / 40% software split in day\-to\-day work

### **Location**


On\-site in **New York, NY, USA**. Candidates must be willing to work in\-person.
